channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Officials work to uncover the motive for fire set at Pennsylvania governor's residence
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Investigators worked Tuesday to uncover the motive behind an arson fire over the weekend at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s mansion, the latest act of political violence in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: People detained in Myanmar after release from scam compounds attempt an escape
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — They walked out of the compound in Myanmar not knowing where they would go. Though they were aiming for the river that separated them from Thailand and freedom, they didn't know if they would make it across.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US flies long-range bomber for drill with South Korea, as the North marks a key state anniversary
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The United States flew long-range B-1B bombers in a show of force against North Korea on Tuesday, days after the powerful sister of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un vowed to resist a U.S.-led push to eliminate the North’s nuclear program. North Korea often reacts to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jury selection to begin in Harvey Weinstein's retrial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The last time a New York City jury sat in judgment of Harvey Weinstein, the ex-movie studio boss was convicted of rape and sentenced to 23 years in prison.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Study finds more people are obtaining abortions but fewer are traveling to other states for it
item.description: Fewer people crossed state lines to obtain abortions in 2024 than a year earlier, a new survey has found.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch: Elephants at San Diego park form protective circle during earthquake
item.description: Watch: Elephants at the San Diego Zoo Safari Park surrounded the younger elephants in a protective "alert circle" when a 5.2 magnitude earthquake struck the region Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former Colorado deputy gets 3 years in prison for fatally shooting man who called for help
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former Colorado sheriff’s deputy convicted of killing a 22-year-old man in distress who called 911 for help was sentenced Monday to three years in prison, the maximum sentence, by a judge who said the shooting was about power.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Immigration judges sue over mass firings at DOJ
item.description: A group of recently appointed immigration judges who were fired by mass layouts have filed a lawsuit against the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Video shows mother pick up baby and run during Southern California earthquake
item.description: A quick-thinking San Diego County mother saved her baby from a 5.2 magnitude earthquake in eastern San Diego County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KTLA 5

item.title: Diddy pleads not guilty to additional charges in new indictment
item.description: The music mogul faces federal charges of racketeering, sex trafficking and of transportation to engage in prostitution.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KTLA 5

item.title: Phone calls, police interview footage shared during Lori Vallow Daybell murder trial
item.description: PHOENIX (ABC4) -- The Idaho mother convicted of killing her two children returned to the courtroom Monday on charges of conspiracy to murder her husband, Charles Vallow. Lori Vallow Daybell is representing herself in Maricopa County during this trial.
item.dc:creator: Sorina Trauntvein, ABC 4

item.title: Seven US service members had ‘COVID-19-like symptoms’ after 2019 Wuhan games: Pentagon report
item.description: Seven U.S. service members exhibited “COVID-19-like symptoms” during or after their return from the 2019 World Military Games in Wuhan, China, according to a Pentagon report recently made public.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge denies bail for man accused of setting fire at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro's residence
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A man who allegedly scaled an iron security fence in the middle of the night, eluded police and set fire to the Pennsylvania governor’s mansion was denied bail Monday as he faced charges including attempted homicide, terrorism and arson.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Massive bluefin tuna catches halted at pier after officials step in, manager says
item.description: For several days a huge bluefin tuna were caught at Jennette's Pier in Nags Head, but the bluefin fun came to an end after an official visited last week, the pier said.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FEMA denies Washington state disaster relief from bomb cyclone, governor says
item.description: The Federal Emergency Management Agency has denied Washington state's request for emergency relief funds to help repair an estimated $34 million in damage from a deadly bomb cyclone storm system in November, according to Gov. Bob Ferguson.
item.dc:creator: LISA BAUMANN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AG: Man charged for AI-generated child sex material
item.description: “This proactive investigation uncovered a defendant weaponizing modern technology to victimize and harm children,” Attorney General said.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Thompson, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department charges man with arson at New Mexico Tesla dealership and GOP headquarters
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department has charged a man with vandalizing a Tesla dealership in New Mexico and setting fire to the headquarters of the state Republican Party, according to court records unsealed Monday. A criminal complaint charges Jamison R.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York plane crash claims lives of high-achieving family celebrating a birthday
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — When they boarded a small plane bound for upstate New York this past weekend, the six passengers — a pair of close-knit siblings, their parents and partners — were on the cusp of a new chapter in a family life already replete with personal and professional accomplishment.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: ICE, ATF capture fugitive in multinational manhunt wanted for 'brutal murder' in the Caribbean
item.description: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement announced the capture of a fugitive, wanted in connection to a 2020 murder, who escaped custody twice in the Caribbean.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A celestial 'smiley face' will be visible to stargazers later this month: What to know
item.description: Stargazers who turn their eyes skyward — and use a little bit of imagination — will have a chance to spot a rare "smiley face" in space later this month.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, WGN 9

item.title: Lawmakers push to meet with Salvadoran president to plead case of mistakenly deported man
item.description: Lawmakers are pushing to meet with Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele while he’s in Washington amid broader efforts to secure the return of a Maryland man wrongfully deported to a prison in the Central American nation. Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D-Md.) sent a letter to El Salvador’s ambassador to the U.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: American college students on spring break released from jail after alleged dispute with Uber driver
item.description: Two U.S. college students have been released from jail in Denmark after an alleged altercation with an Uber driver while the pair were traveling for spring break.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect arsonist was prepared to attack Pennsylvania Gov. Shapiro with hammer, court docs reveal
item.description: The man charged in connection with a fire at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro's residence was reportedly prepared to attack him.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, Fox 5

item.title: 2022 NCAA woman of the year, family members killed in upstate New York plane crash
item.description: A private plane that crashed in upstate New York over the weekend was carrying six members of a close-knit family of physicians and distinguished student-athletes on a trip to the Catskills for a birthday celebration and the Passover holiday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NYC helicopter tour company shuts down operations after crash that killed 6
item.description: The helicopter, a Bell 206 L-4, crashed into the Hudson River Thursday, killing all six people aboard.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, KRON4

item.title: Stocks rally worldwide after Trump eases some of his tariffs on electronics, for now
item.description: Stocks rose worldwide Monday after President Donald Trump relaxed some of his tariffs, for now at least, and as stress from within the U.S. bond market seems to be easing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: All-female Blue Origin spaceflight launches from Texas
item.description: Six women are scheduled Monday to take the first all-female flight into space in over half a century.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Meta faces historic antitrust trial that could force it to break off Instagram, WhatsApp
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. faces a historic antitrust trial beginning Monday that could force the tech giant to break off Instagram and WhatsApp, startups it bought more than a decade ago that have since grown into social media powerhouses.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY AP Technology Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Coast Guard rescues 4 passengers from capsized boat off Florida coast; 1 dead, 5 still missing
item.description: A search for five missing passengers who were on a boat that capsized off the coast of Florida remains active after authorities rescued four passengers and found one dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Search continues for Las Vegas veterinarian who apologized for kicking horse
item.description: Earlier this month, Dr. Frehner apologized for kicking a horse he was hired to give shots to. Days later, he was reported missing in the Lake Mead Recreation Area.
item.dc:creator: Madina Ansary, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump's commerce secretary says new electronics tariff exemptions are temporary, chip tariffs coming
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Tariff exemptions announced Friday on electronics like smartphones and laptops are only a temporary reprieve until the Trump administration develops a new tariff approach specific to the semiconductor industry, U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US still won't say whether it will return mistakenly deported man, despite Supreme Court decision
item.description: The Trump administration is doubling down on its decision not to tell a federal court whether it has any plans to repatriate a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported last month and remains confined in a notorious prison in El Salvador, despite a Supreme Court ruling and lower court order that the
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump 'fully fit' to serve as commander in chief, his doctor says after recent physical
item.description: In a report released Sunday, the doctor said in a summary that Trump is “fully fit to execute the duties of Commander-in-Chief and Head of State.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fire at Pennsylvania Governor's Residence was 'act of arson': State Police
item.description: Police said Governor Josh Shapiro and his family were able to safely evacuate after a fire — believed to be an "act of arson" — broke out at the Harrisburg mansion.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, ABC 4

item.title: Nicky Katt of 'Dazed and Confused,' 'Boston Public' dies: reports
item.description: Nicky Katt, who was known for his roles in "Dazed and Confused" and "Boiler Room," has died, according to multiple reports. He was 54 years old.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: No, Casey Anthony did not open a Branson daycare
item.description: Google Trends showed a variety of topics being searched in Missouri on Thursday morning---sports, weather, the lottery, and one specific question: Did Casey Anthony open a daycare in Branson, Missouri?
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, WGN 9
